bugs.description: Hardcoded default API key 'sk-supportai-test' used when SUPPORTAI_API_KEYS env var is not set. Any client can authenticate with this well-known key in production.
bugs.suggestion: Remove hardcoded default; require SUPPORTAI_API_KEYS to be explicitly set, or fail closed (empty list) if not configured.

bugs.description: User message interpolated directly into LLM system prompt via f-string without sanitization. Attacker can inject commands via crafted messages in classify(), analyze_sentiment(), and generate_response().
bugs.suggestion: Apply input sanitization boundary — add separator markers, strip known injection patterns (ignore, disregard, role-play), or use a secondary classifier to detect prompt injection before forwarding.

bugs.description: All database methods use synchronous SQLAlchemy session (SessionLocal) inside async methods. Each DB call blocks the event loop — no asyncio.to_thread() or async SQLAlchemy engine used.
bugs.suggestion: Replace SessionLocal with async SQLAlchemy (create_async_engine, AsyncSession) or wrap each DB call in asyncio.to_thread().

bugs.description: Zero-shot classification model runs synchronously inside async pipeline. Blocking the event loop for multiple seconds per request will degrade throughput under load.
bugs.suggestion: Offload model inference to a thread pool via asyncio.to_thread() or use an async-compatible inference server.

bugs.description: SentenceTransformer .encode() runs synchronously inside async pipeline. Blocks event loop during embedding computation.
bugs.suggestion: Wrap .encode() calls in asyncio.to_thread() or use a pre-computed embedding server.

bugs.description: Transformer sentiment pipeline runs synchronously inside async pipeline. Blocks event loop.
bugs.suggestion: Offload sentiment inference to asyncio.to_thread() or batch processing.

bugs.description: SessionManager stores sessions in an in-memory dict with no expiry mechanism. Sessions never expire — session_expiry_minutes config in PipelineConfig is never consumed. Over time this leaks memory.
bugs.suggestion: Add timestamp-based expiry check in get() and get_or_create(); evict sessions older than configured TTL.

bugs.description: No ticket deduplication check. Multiple messages at escalation_level >= 2 from the same session will create multiple tickets. Session.active_ticket_id exists in DB but is never populated.
bugs.suggestion: Check session.active_ticket_id before creating a new ticket; skip creation if a ticket already exists for the session.

bugs.description: escalation_state column has check constraint with valid states but SessionManager and pipeline never update it. The state machine exists in schema but has no runtime implementation.
bugs.suggestion: Implement escalation state transitions in the pipeline after _compute_escalation() — persist to session table.

bugs.description: Chat widget POSTs to '/api/chat/init' and '/api/chat/message' — neither endpoint exists. The actual chat endpoint is POST /api/chat. Widget session init silently fails (catch block is empty).
bugs.suggestion: Update widget to call POST /api/chat for messages and remove or implement the /api/chat/init endpoint.

bugs.description: Hardcoded length check (1000) in _validate_response ignores the max_chars parameter. If max_chars is configured differently (e.g., 2000), validation will reject valid LLM responses.
bugs.suggestion: Use self._max_chars or pass max_chars to _validate_response instead of hardcoding 1000.

bugs.description: Swagger docs (/docs), ReDoc (/redoc), OpenAPI JSON, and root path (/) are exempt from authentication. Docs expose full API surface without auth.
bugs.suggestion: Require auth for /docs and /redoc in production, or gate behind an admin-only condition.

bugs.description: Reads SessionManager._sessions directly (private class-level attribute). This references the class attribute, not instance state. Fragile and bypasses any future session store refactoring.
bugs.suggestion: Add a classmethod or property to SessionManager for session count — e.g., SessionManager.active_count().

bugs.description: Message.faq_entry uses legacy backref pattern ('messages_using_faq') while all other relationships use modern back_populates. Inconsistent and may cause silent failures if session/bidirectional sync is used.
bugs.suggestion: Replace backref='messages_using_faq' with back_populates paired field on FAQEntry.

bugs.description: Session.negative_message_count column has no CHECK constraint ensuring >= 0. If decremented below zero, no integrity error is raised.
No unique or partial-unique constraint on Ticket.session_id. Multiple tickets for the same session are allowed at schema level.
bugs.suggestion: Consider a partial unique constraint on session_id where status != 'resolved', or enforce at application layer.

bugs.description: Lifespan handler reads request_id_header from app.state as 'request_id', but no request_id has been set on app.state at that point — always returns None.
bugs.suggestion: Remove this line or set a default such as app.state.request_id = 'startup' before referencing it.
